title: Sinclair gets FDA clearance for first human gene-therapy trial reversing cellular aging
description: David Sinclair's Life Biosciences cleared FDA review to begin a first-in-human trial of a partial-reprogramming gene therapy designed to reverse cellular aging, starting with glaucoma and non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy (NAION). The clearance is the first time a pure partial-reprogramming therapy has been authorized for direct human aging-target use in the United States — a major milestone for the Information Theory of Aging.

title: Frontiers in Aging Neuroscience: semaglutide neuroprotection in cognitive dysfunction
description: A Frontiers in Aging Neuroscience review consolidates evidence that semaglutide and other GLP-1 receptor agonists exert direct neuroprotective effects in cognitive dysfunction, layering brain-aging evidence on top of UC San Diego's DunedinPACE 9% slowdown result. The combination is rapidly converting GLP-1s from metabolic drugs into front-line cognitive-aging interventions.

title: Fight Aging: Bat-inspired Core Longevity State Vector (CLSV-6) reframes inflammaging
description: A new Fight Aging review proposes the Core Longevity State Vector (CLSV-6), drawn from bat immunology: damage tolerance, autophagy-mitophagy, proteostasis, basal immune readiness, inflammasome regulation, and inflammatory resolution capacity. Human centenarians converge toward this bat-like configuration, and the framework anchors functional foods (polyphenols, fermented foods, herbal extracts) as a practical implementation pathway.

title: Crunchbase: NewLimit's $435M Series C lands in the top 10 funding rounds of the week
description: Crunchbase's weekly biggest-rounds report ranks NewLimit's $435M Series C as the 5th-largest funding round globally this week — the first time a pure longevity company has reached top-tier weekly visibility outside biotech-only press. The validation places aging reversal squarely in mainstream venture mindshare.

title: Harvard Health Special Report names geroscience, GLP-1s, rapamycin and senolytics as 5-15 year frontiers
description: Harvard Health Publishing's new Special Health Report on aging biology positions geroscience, biological aging, the hallmarks framework, GLP-1s, rapamycin, senolytics and biological age testing as the most important preventive-medicine frontiers for the next 5-15 years — while reinforcing lifestyle as the foundation. The report puts Harvard's institutional weight behind a longevity stack that consumer media already covers heavily.

title: David Sinclair: FDA clearance for first human cellular-aging gene therapy
description: Life Biosciences received FDA clearance to begin a first-in-human partial-reprogramming gene-therapy trial designed to reverse cellular aging, starting with glaucoma and NAION — the first true 'reverse aging' trial of its kind in the US.

title: Sinclair at University of Nicosia: 'There is no law of biology that says we have to age'
description: David Sinclair received an honorary doctorate at the University of Nicosia and delivered a lecture arguing that aging is the 'mother of all diseases' and that his Life Biosciences therapy will enter human trials beginning with glaucoma and NAION.

title: Crunchbase weekly funding: NewLimit $435M places longevity in top 5
description: Crunchbase News ranks NewLimit's $435M Series C as the 5th-largest startup round of the week behind Ramp ($750M), Supabase ($500M), Flourish ($500M) and Helion ($465M). Founders Fund led the financing for the South San Francisco company.

title: Fitt Insider: NewLimit at $3.1B valuation as longevity medicine accelerates
description: Fitt Insider's Health & Wellness Funding News for June 5 highlights NewLimit's $435M Series C at a $3.1B valuation (more than triple last year), naming it the standout longevity raise of the week.

title: Frontiers in Aging Neuroscience: semaglutide neuroprotection in cognitive dysfunction
description: A new Frontiers review consolidates preclinical and clinical evidence that semaglutide exerts neuroprotective effects in cognitive dysfunction, supporting GLP-1 receptor agonists as candidate aging-brain therapeutics.

title: UC San Diego semaglutide RCT — DunedinPACE -9% — continues to drive global coverage
description: UCSD's Nature Communications RCT in 108 adults with HIV-associated lipohypertrophy showed semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE biological aging by 9% and reduced PCGrimAge mortality markers across multiple organ-specific clocks.

title: Fight Aging: bat-inspired Core Longevity State Vector (CLSV-6)
description: Fight Aging covers a new CLSV-6 framework — damage tolerance, autophagy-mitophagy, proteostasis, basal immune readiness, inflammasome regulation, inflammatory resolution — drawn from bat immunology, with human centenarians converging toward this bat-like immunotype.

title: Fight Aging: gut microbe Bacteroides fragilis increases sepsis risk and severity
description: Fight Aging reports new microbiome research identifying a gut microbe (Bacteroides fragilis) that increases both the risk and severity of sepsis in older adults — adding a longevity-relevant target for microbiome modulation.

title: UCLA longevity research panel showcases biological-age tools and stem-cell rejuvenation
description: UCLA's longevity research panel — including Broad Stem Cell Research Center director Dr. Thomas Rando — showcased DNA-based biological-age clocks, glutathione-enabled muscle stem-cell rejuvenation, young-blood / exercise-blood transfer effects in mice, and partial-reprogramming gene work.

title: Harvard Health Publishing Special Report: aging biology, hallmarks, GLP-1s, rapamycin, senolytics
description: Harvard Health Publishing's new Special Health Report covers geroscience, the hallmarks of aging framework, GLP-1s, rapamycin, senolytics and biological age testing as the most important preventive-medicine frontiers for the next 5-15 years.

title: AGE2026 Day 1 (Kaeberlein on LinkedIn): mTOR, rapamycin, ovarian and immune aging dominate
description: Matt Kaeberlein's LinkedIn recap of AGE2026 Day 1 highlights mTOR/rapamycin's continued dominance, including new immune, inflammation, ovarian-aging, brain and metabolism data.

title: Rhonda Patrick 2026 updated supplement list (with brands)
description: Fast Life Hacks publishes the latest Rhonda Patrick supplement list with brand recommendations, dosages and 2026 updates — a long-running reference protocol in the longevity community.

title: NewLimit $435M Series C: $3.1B valuation, Founders Fund + Thrive + Lilly Ventures
description: NewLimit's $435M Series C lifted its valuation to $3.1B — more than triple a year ago — with Founders Fund, Thrive Capital and Lilly Ventures on the cap table. CEO Jacob Kimmel framed liver disease as a stepping stone to broader anti-aging applications.

title: Senescence News digest June 3: vgll3 trade-off, sleep + aging, GLP-1 knee replacement
description: This week's Senescence News digest highlights vgll3 (a gene promoting rapid growth and early reproduction while raising cancer and aging risk), sleep problems in mid-life women predicting poorer psychological well-being 10 years later, and GLP-1s associated with lower long-term knee-replacement risk.

title: D+Q senolytic combo causes severe brain demyelination in mice (UConn)
description: UConn researchers report that dasatinib + quercetin at doses used in longevity experiments triggered profound myelin loss in the corpus callosum of both young and old mice, mimicking MS or chemo brain — a stark safety warning for off-label D+Q protocols.

title: ARTAN Bio raises $200K via VitaDAO for nonsense-mutation suppressor platform
description: ARTAN Bio raised $200K in decentralized seed funding via VitaDAO to advance ARTAN-102, an engineered tRNA suppressor targeting premature stop codons in aging-related and genetic diseases.

title: Rapamycin in endometriosis and fertility — Columbia VIBRANT + Cell Reports Medicine IVF RCT
description: ESSI clinical synthesis integrates Columbia's VIBRANT trial (~20% slower follicular loss) and the Li et al. Cell Reports Medicine IVF RCT (clinical pregnancy 50.0% rapamycin vs 28.2% control) into pulsed rapamycin protocols for ovarian aging and recurrent IVF failure.

title: Two national canine antiaging clinical trials: rapamycin and LOY-002
description: Two national clinical trials of antiaging drugs are underway in dogs — rapamycin and Loyal's LOY-002 — with positive healthspan readouts expected to bolster the case for cross-species use.

title: NMN.com: PEARL trial — first long-term human rapamycin RCT shows muscle and bone benefits
description: PEARL reports no serious adverse events across 48 weeks of weekly rapamycin in older adults; 10 mg/week preserved muscle in women and bone mineral content in men.

title: Oxford Aging Cell: rapamycin protects T cells from DNA damage
description: Oxford's Kell lab reports that low-dose rapamycin reduces DNA double-strand breaks in T cells, doubles their survival after damage and lowers senescence markers in older adults — adding immune rejuvenation to mTOR's healthspan story.

title: University of Sydney 4-week diet RCT reduces biological age in 65-75 y.o.
description: A 104-person Aging Cell RCT shows three of four dietary patterns reduced biological-age scores after just 4 weeks in adults aged 65-75; higher-carb omnivore arm produced the largest gain.

title: Sinclair + Adiv Johnson evidence map: which interventions actually slow epigenetic aging in humans
description: The Sinclair/Adiv Johnson Frontiers in Genetics evidence map analyzes 41 human studies and identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diets, caloric restriction, omega-3s and multivitamins as the interventions with positive next-generation epigenetic clock signal.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1: oxidized cholesterol excretion first-in-human
description: Cyclarity Therapeutics presented Phase 1 data at AHA Vascular Discovery showing UDP-003 produced measurable 7-ketocholesterol excretion in humans — the first clinical proof of concept for clearing oxidized cholesterol from soft plaques.

title: Healthcare Discovery AI: Daily Rounds Longevity & Healthcare Brief
description: Daily Rounds longevity brief synthesizes the week's most relevant geroscience, biotech and protocol news for clinicians and longevity-focused operators.

title: Peter Attia Drive #394: sleep pharmacology and Alzheimer's prevention
description: Attia's new podcast episode dives into sleep pharmacology, prescription sleep meds and their effects on sleep architecture, and dual orexin receptor antagonists (DORAs) for Alzheimer's prevention in high-risk patients.
